title: RDBMS vs MongoDB Terminology Comparison
description: A side-by-side visual comparison of key terminology used in traditional Relational Database Management Systems (RDBMS) versus MongoDB, a NoSQL document database.
elements: RDBMS Terms: Table, Row, Column, Schema, Primary Key, Foreign Key
MongoDB Terms: Collection, Document, Field, BSON Structure, _id Field, Reference/Embedding
relationships: RDBMS Table is analogous to MongoDB Collection
RDBMS Row is analogous to MongoDB Document
RDBMS Column is analogous to MongoDB Field
RDBMS Schema defines structure, MongoDB has dynamic schema
RDBMS uses Joins with Foreign Keys, MongoDB uses Referencing or Embedding
